*Table: This table compiles 16 high-value references spanning code-based CAD, multi-agent assembly, geometric validation, surveys, and spatial reasoning limitations. It highlights the most decision-relevant quantitative results available from the gathered evidence, especially execution success, exact-match, invalid-rate, Chamfer-distance, and feasibility outcomes.*
artifacts.artifact-01:
| Mitigation Axis | Strategy | Representative Reference(s) | How it Works (1-2 sentences) | Reported Effectiveness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A — Tool-Side Guardrails | OCCT kernel topology checks | CADSmith (Barkley et al., 2026) (pqac-00000001, pqac-00000011) | The pipeline checks executable CadQuery/OCCT outputs against deterministic geometry facts: bounding-box dimensions, volume, face/edge/vertex counts, and solid validity, then uses these signals in an outer repair loop. This catches millimeter-scale dimensional and topology errors that image-only judging can miss. | 100% execution success; median F1 improved from 0.9707 to 0.9846; median IoU from 0.8085 to 0.9629; mean Chamfer Distance from 28.37 to 0.74 (pqac-00000001) |
| A — Tool-Side Guardrails | DPO with code-checker feedback | CADCrafter (Chen et al., 2025) (pqac-00000029) | CADCrafter fine-tunes its generative model using automatic CAD code checker feedback as a preference signal, so geometrically invalid or non-compiling sequences are penalized during training. This injects non-differentiable CAD validity signals into learning. | On DeepCAD multi-view, Invalid Rate = 0.036 and Median Chamfer Distance = 0.026; on RealCAD multi-view, IR = 0.067 and Med CD = 0.062 (pqac-00000029) |
| A — Tool-Side Guardrails | Self-repair diffusion pipeline | GenCAD-Self-Repairing (Tsuji et al., 2025) (pqac-00000033) | Infeasible command sequences are identified by failed B-rep construction, then repaired by latent-space diffusion guidance plus a regressor that maps infeasible latent vectors toward feasible ones. The corrected latent is decoded again to improve geometry feasibility. | Feasibility improved from 0.931 to 0.970; repaired 65.84% of baseline infeasible cases (532/808) (pqac-00000033) |
| A — Tool-Side Guardrails | Watertightness / manifold validity checks | AI-driven CAD survey (Wu et al., 2026); CADEvolve discussion (pqac-00000014, pqac-00000004) | Surveyed work emphasizes explicit checks for self-intersections, gaps, non-manifold edges, non-watertight solids, and degenerate outputs before downstream manufacturing, slicing, or FEA. These checks are proposed as basic admissibility filters for generated CAD. | Survey identifies non-watertight and non-manifold outputs as a major blocker for manufacturing/FEA; CADEvolve treats invalid generations as a first-class error category in training/evaluation (pqac-00000014, pqac-00000004) |
| B — Prompting / Agent Strategies | Multi-agent with independent VLM judge | CADSmith (Barkley et al., 2026) (pqac-00000008, pqac-00000000) | CADSmith decomposes work across Planner, Coder, Executor, Validator, and Refiner agents, with an independent vision-language Judge that sees code, OCCT metrics, and three-view renders. This critic-revisor architecture reduces self-confirmation bias and supports iterative repair. | Full pipeline produced 100% execution success and ~38× lower mean Chamfer Distance than zero-shot; removing renders increased T3 mean CD from 1.42 to 49.68 (pqac-00000000, pqac-00000008) |
| B — Prompting / Agent Strategies | Visual feedback VQA loop | CADCodeVerify (Alrashedy et al., 2025) (pqac-00000030) | A VLM generates validation questions about rendered geometry, answers them from the image, and uses the resulting feedback to revise CAD code. This creates a visual chain-of-thought / critic loop anchored in rendered shape rather than text alone. | GPT-4 few-shot success rate improved from 96.0% to 96.5%; point-cloud distance improved from 0.155 to 0.127; IoGT rose to 0.944 (pqac-00000030) |
| B — Prompting / Agent Strategies | Multi-agent role-based design | From Idea to CAD (Ocker et al., 2025) (pqac-00000009, pqac-00000037) | The system uses specialized RequirementsEngineer, CadEngineer, and QualityAssuranceEngineer roles, plus a verification loop over seven rendered views and an outer human validation loop. The decomposition mirrors real engineering workflows and helps localize errors. | Reported qualitatively to outperform a 0-shot VLM baseline across example tasks; authors note higher readiness/compliance but also persistent failures on orientation/workplane selection for complex parts (pqac-00000037) |
| B — Prompting / Agent Strategies | Design-stage connector planning for assemblies | ArtiCAD (Shui et al., 2026) (pqac-00000036) | Instead of asking the model to infer assembly relationships after generating all part code, ArtiCAD predicts connectors, local frames, semantic labels, and joint limits up front. Final assembly then reduces to deterministic alignment with a constraint solver, bypassing long-context spatial reasoning at assembly time. | Introduces a 120-task benchmark and explicitly argues early connector prediction avoids the high failure rate of late assembly inference; quantitative benchmark details were not in extracted pages (pqac-00000036) |
| B — Prompting / Agent Strategies | Structured error feedback | CADDesigner / ECIP (Fan et al., 2025) (pqac-00000035) | ECIP replaces implicit CadQuery state with explicit state passing and emits structured error triples: (cause, location, corrective action). This gives the model machine-readable debugging feedback instead of vague runtime traces. | Reported as improving modularity, readability, and ease of debugging for agent-driven CAD generation; no scalar benchmark extracted from the cited pages (pqac-00000035) |
| B — Prompting / Agent Strategies | RL fine-tuning with online feedback | cadrille; ReACT survey context (pqac-00000016) | Recent CAD generation systems treat CAD sequence prediction as a sequential decision problem and optimize with reward signals tied to geometric fidelity and validity, rather than imitation alone. This is intended to improve long-horizon geometric decisions. | Survey identifies RL-based CAD generation as a key emerging direction; extracted pages note state-of-the-art gains but do not provide exact cadrille numbers in the available evidence (pqac-00000016) |
| C — Representation Choices | CadQuery / Python code instead of task-specific command tokens | Text-to-CadQuery; CAD-Coder (pqac-00000031, pqac-00000032) | Direct Python/CadQuery generation leverages pretrained code-generation ability and exposes procedural logic, assertions, and debugging affordances unavailable in compact task-specific tokenizations. It also aligns with existing software stacks and RAG over API docs. | Text-to-CadQuery reached 69.30% top-1 exact match, F1 median 0.9836, IoU median 0.9868; CAD-Coder reports 100% valid syntax rate (pqac-00000031, pqac-00000032) |
| C — Representation Choices | Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm (ECIP) | CADDesigner (Fan et al., 2025) (pqac-00000035) | ECIP replaces chained, implicitly stateful CadQuery calls with explicit input/output passing for every operation. That makes dependencies visible, permits normal Python control flow, and simplifies both debugging and code review. | Reported to improve code clarity, flexibility, modularity, and interpretability for LLM agents; benchmark numbers were not extracted in the cited pages (pqac-00000035) |
| C — Representation Choices | Direct B-rep generation with explicit topology | GraphBrep / HiDiGen; representation surveys (pqac-00000017, pqac-00000016) | B-rep generation models operate on explicit faces, edges, vertices, and topology graphs rather than only sketch-extrude command histories. This can avoid fixed-vocabulary command bottlenecks but raises topology-learning complexity. | Surveys describe direct B-rep synthesis as a promising alternative to restricted sketch-extrude sequences, especially for complex geometry/topology; no single effectiveness number was extracted for GraphBrep/HiDiGen from the available pages (pqac-00000017, pqac-00000016) |
| C — Representation Choices | Dataset/benchmark choice | DeepCAD, ABC, Fusion 360 Gallery, SketchGraphs (pqac-00000016, pqac-00000019) | Representation and evaluation quality are bounded by dataset coverage: DeepCAD provides command sequences, ABC provides large-scale B-reps, Fusion 360 Gallery provides human-authored build histories, and SketchGraphs provides constrained 2D sketches. Choosing the dataset determines whether the model learns code, topology, constraints, or edit history. | Reported sizes: DeepCAD 178,238; ABC 1M+; Fusion 360 Gallery 8,625 (or ~8.6K); SketchGraphs 15M (pqac-00000016, pqac-00000019) |
| D — Human-in-the-Loop | Human validation loop after automated verification | From Idea to CAD (Ocker et al., 2025) (pqac-00000037) | After a VLM-based QA loop, the user is shown the candidate model and can provide specific corrective feedback; the system then regenerates the model using that feedback. This inserts a human checkpoint after automated geometric critique. | Demonstrated iterative improvements on examples such as wheel orientation/width corrections; authors position this as necessary because the system may fail to converge autonomously (pqac-00000037) |
| D — Human-in-the-Loop | Quantitative HITL evaluation pipeline | Sadik & Bujny (2025) (pqac-00000007, pqac-00000028) | The human-in-the-loop process evaluates outputs with volumetric similarity, surface similarity, Hausdorff distance, PCA alignment, ICP alignment, and a weighted final score, then iteratively revises prompts/code until convergence. This turns manual review into a measurable optimization loop. | Code-level correction prompts achieved perfect or near-perfect metrics in the cited case, including zero Hausdorff distance; paper argues structured metrics are necessary for engineering-grade review (pqac-00000007, pqac-00000028) |
| D — Human-in-the-Loop | Review agent + user review of assemblies | ArtiCAD (Shui et al., 2026) (pqac-00000036) | ArtiCAD’s Review Agent inspects multi-view renders of each part plus keyframe sequences of articulated joint motions, then feeds findings back into responsible upstream agents via rollback. A user can then judge the resulting assembly. | Presented as a practical safeguard for multi-part articulated assemblies; quantitative review-stage gains were not in extracted pages (pqac-00000036) |
| D — Human-in-the-Loop | Code-level correction as strongest supervision | Sadik & Bujny (2025) (pqac-00000007, pqac-00000028) | Their experiments compare low-information inputs (orthographic views, sketches) to higher-information symbolic/code-level prompts, showing that direct access to parametric/source-level information drastically lowers edit distance and ambiguity. This suggests manual edits should feed back into the parametric source, not only into rendered geometry. | Code-level prompts achieved near-perfect reconstruction across metrics and outperformed image-only inputs; cited case reports zero Hausdorff distance (pqac-00000007, pqac-00000028) |


*Table: This table organizes mitigations for LLM-generated mechanical CAD across tool-side guardrails, agent/prompting strategies, representation choices, and human-in-the-loop workflows. It highlights how each mitigation works and, where available, the reported empirical gains.*

info.query: Find recent (2023-2026) peer-reviewed and arXiv academic publications, engineering reports, and developer write-ups on the spatial reasoning, assembly, and geometric-correctness limitations of large language models (LLMs) and agentic systems when generating mechanical CAD — specifically code-based CAD (CadQuery, OpenSCAD, build123d, FreeCAD Python API), B-rep / feature-tree generation, and LLM-driven parametric assembly. Cover the following failure modes that have been empirically observed in Claude Opus 4.7 / GPT-class models generating CadQuery code: (i) parts modelled in isolation but never Boolean-unioned with the host body (e.g., mounting bosses floating off a ring, brackets not attached); (ii) wrong-direction Boolean cuts and extrudes (e.g., CadQuery `workplane(invert=True)`, `extrude(both=True)` doubling thickness, `cut`/`hole` going the wrong way); (iii) clearance / fit / tolerance values that are arithmetically inconsistent (e.g., N modules on a pitch circle that geometrically overlap, made-up clearances that don't match operating envelope); (iv) missing functional paths (e.g., no inlet for powder/fluid, motor blocks the part it drives, no fastener access); (v) vendor-part envelopes ignored or misremembered; (vi) fastener strategy underspecified (self-tap into FDM print vs. heat-set inserts vs. through-bolts). For each source give: full citation (authors, year, venue, DOI/arXiv ID), 3-5 sentence summary, and the concrete mitigation strategy it proposes or evaluates. Specifically catalogue mitigations across these axes: (A) tool-side guardrails — schema-constrained decoding, deterministic geometry-kernel verifiers (OCCT/CadQuery topology checks, watertight/manifold checks, interference / clash detection, DRC-style design rule checks for AM, FEA-in-the-loop, slicer-side printability checks); (B) prompting / agent strategies — self-consistency, self-repair / critic-revisor loops, ReAct-style tool-use with a CAD kernel, multi-view rendering fed back into the model (visual chain-of-thought / image-augmented critique), test-driven CAD (write assertions on volume, mass, bounding box, hole counts, interference-free assemblies); (C) representation choices — sketch + constraint-based vs. CSG vs. mesh, decomposition into sub-assemblies, explicit interface contracts between parts, parametric skeletons / datums, vendor-component libraries with verified envelopes (KiCad-style footprint libs but for mechanical); (D) human-in-the-loop and manual-intervention patterns — where to insert a human reviewer, what review artifacts (multi-view renders, dimensioned 2D sketches, exploded views, animated assembly), how to make manual edits feed back into the parametric source, and the cost/benefit of fully autonomous vs. supervised generation for hardware that will be 3D printed and assembled. Include landmark / canonical references (CAD-LLM, Text2CAD, DeepCAD, SkexGen, Fusion 360 Gallery, ABC dataset, SketchGraphs, CADCrafter, BlenderBench, related agentic-design benchmarks like BlocksWorld / spatial-reasoning evals) and quote any reported quantitative results (pass rate, watertightness rate, manufacturability rate, human edit distance). Aim for ~12-18 references.
